text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Mental & Behavioral Health > Child Sexual Abuse Counseling 5 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF BALANCE CENTER FOR CHILDREN AND FAMILIES BILINGUAL MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides bilingual mental health services. Specialties include trauma, prenatal and postpartum depression and anxiety, at-risk adolescents, depression, anxiety, parenting support, acculturation, and immigration issues. 11740 SW Warner Avenue Tigard, OR 97223 Eligibility: Unrestricted, with a focus on Hispanic and Latinx communities Hours: By appointment only Email (503) 596-2222 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIBERTY HOUSE HOPE & WELLNESS SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides counseling to youth and their families who have received an assessment for abuse or neglect at Liberty House or another child abuse assessment center; or who have experienced trauma or grief. Services are trauma-focused with an emphasis on collaboration with the client and family to develop and work toward goals. 2685 4th Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Ages 0-18 facing concerns of child abuse, neglect, trauma, or grief, and their families or caregivers Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 689-1006 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES ADMIN OFFICE COUNSELING PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers outpatient mental health treatment. Therapists can address a range of mental, emotional, behavioral, and situational issues, including family conflict, physical and sexual abuse, trauma, grief, adjustment problems, co-occurring disorders, ADD/ADHD, school problems, domestic violence, sexual acting out, socialization problems, self-destructive behaviors, depression, anxiety/stress, anger management, self-esteem issues, relationship problems, and emotional problems. 1790 W 11th Avenue Suite 200 Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Unrestricted; varies by program Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-6pm, Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 484-4428 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES SPRINGFIELD COUNSELING PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers outpatient mental health treatment.
